name: Dick Gregory
bio: Dick Gregory was an American comedian and civil rights activist who broke barriers as one of the first Black stand-ups to win a mainstream white audience, delivering pointed, deadpan material on race and current events. His 1961 breakthrough at the Chicago Playboy Club made him a national figure, and he increasingly merged his comedy with political activism throughout the 1960s.

timeline.ev: Breakthrough run at the Chicago Playboy Club launches his national career
Makes his New York debut at the Blue Angel and releases early albums
Runs for U.S. President as a write-in candidate
Publishes Dick Gregorys Natural Diet for Folks Who Eat

accolades: Among the first Black comedians to perform for mainstream white audiences
Ranked No. 82 on Comedy Centrals 100 Greatest Stand-ups of All Time
Has a star on the St. Louis Walk of Fame
Autobiography sold over a million copies
